title: tomophyllum lividum
intro: Tomophyllum lividum, also known as the liverwort, is a small, non-vascular plant species belonging to the family Lejeuneaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Tomophyllum lividum include the treatment of various ailments such as skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate pain. Tomophyllum lividum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, and is also used in traditional medicine in various forms, including ointments and poultices.
